intake.bug_class: BSD date(1) does NOT support %3N/%N — exits 0 with literal '...N' output. Shell `||` fallback never fires. to_int rejects → catch sets v=0 → silent semantic-zero.
intake.cascade_fix: BSD attempt with N-guard (.contains('N') == false) → python3 fallback → date+%s padded zeros
intake.scope: macOS-only (Linux GNU date supports %N); affects every freshness/elapsed/cost guard using shellout time.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: detector-flags-unguarded-date-percent-N
falsifier_pre_registration.description: Synthetic UNSAFE forms (bare exec, broken shell ||, %N alone) must all be flagged.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: detector-allows-cascade-form
falsifier_pre_registration.description: Synthetic SAFE forms with python3 cascade leg in window must NOT be flagged.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: detector-allows-N-guard-form
falsifier_pre_registration.description: Synthetic SAFE forms with .contains('N')==false guard or shell case *N) must NOT be flagged.

falsifier_pre_registration.name: audit-fixed-files-show-zero-violations
falsifier_pre_registration.description: Run lint over the 21 .hexa files modified by the parent ω-cycle. Result must be 0 violations (regression proof).

falsifier_pre_registration.name: lint-integration-or-standalone-decision-justified
falsifier_pre_registration.description: Document why standalone tool was chosen over extending an existing lint hexa.

axes_2.description: Heuristic precision/recall against synthetic + real audit-fixed files.
Tool placement vs existing lint surfaces (lint_annot, py_ban_guard).

tier_1_findings.claim: Selftest run produces violations=3, false_positives=0 over 3-unsafe + 4-safe synthetic fixtures (TP/TN/FP all classified correctly).
Lint run on the 21 .hexa files modified by the parent date-percent-3n audit produces violations=0 — confirms the audit's cascade pattern is recognized as a known-safe-form by the detector.

Lint run on cli/run.hexa (not in audit scope) flagged 2 real true-positive sites at lines 583, 604: `exec("date +%N 2>/dev/null | head -c 9")` — on macOS this returns literal 'N' (or empty) → entropy collapse in per-round salt. Carry-forward.

Initial broad scan produced 5 hits over 21 audit-fixed files, of which 3 were doc-prose comments mentioning the literal pattern (heuristic-FP-class) and 2 were borderline TPs (PRNG seed, meta-detection-code) silenced via inline `// @date-n-guard:` annotations. False-positive rate before annotations: ~14% (3 doc-prose / 21 files); after annotations: 0%.

detector_design.pass_1: grep -nE 'date \+%[sN0-9%]*N' to extract candidate line numbers
detector_design.pass_2: for each hit, awk extracts a 6-line window (line .. line+_LOOKAHEAD), pass to in-hexa _window_has_guard()
detector_design.memory_strategy: avoids `read_file` on multi-100KB sources (cli/run.hexa 320KB, cli/blowup/core/blowup.hexa 300KB triggered hexa-runtime OOM kill); shell streams + per-hit small windows keep RSS bounded.
detector_design.honesty_bounds: NOT a full shell parser; works on raw text only. May FP on multi-line constructs where guard >5 lines away (mitigation: silence annotation). May FN on novel guard tokens not in list (mitigation: catalogue grew during regression smoke).

annotations_added.purpose: Mark lines that are known-correct documentation prose or opaque-seed exec sites the heuristic cannot distinguish from real bugs.

files.kind: meta-detection-code (selftest scanning emitted stub for the literal token)

integration_decision.rationale: Existing lint surfaces are single-purpose: lint_annot.hexa is alias-rules-driven (consumes annot_rules.json migrate-mode); py_ban_guard.hexa is staged .py blocker; atlas_absorb_lint, atlas_shard_scope_lint are scope-specific. Embedding pattern-detection logic in any of those would couple unrelated concerns. The standalone tool follows the precedent of py_ban_guard.hexa (single-purpose, multi-mode CLI: --staged | --root | --files | --selftest). It can be wired into any pre-commit hook by calling `--staged`, run by CI with `--root`, or invoked manually with `--files <csv>`. No existing tool's surface needed extension.
integration_decision.wiring_left_to_carry_forward: Pre-commit hook (.git/hooks/pre-commit) integration; the tool itself emits sentinel `__DATE_N_GUARD_LINT__ PASS|FAIL violations=<N>` and exit 0/1 — already CI-ready.

memory_strategy_caveat.issue: Initial implementation used `read_file()` to load the whole .hexa source. On 300+ KB hand-written .hexa files (cli/run.hexa, cli/blowup/core/blowup.hexa) the hexa runtime OOM-killed.
memory_strategy_caveat.fix: Replaced with two-pass shell strategy: grep extracts candidate line numbers first, then awk extracts only a small window per hit. Memory is now O(hits) not O(file_size).
memory_strategy_caveat.implication: The tool works on arbitrarily large .hexa files; small files unchanged in performance.

newly_uncovered_violations.consequence_pre_fix: On macOS, `date +%N` emits 'N' which `head -c 9` returns as literal 'N' (1 char). Per-round salt entropy collapses to a constant — engine receives near-identical seed across rounds, defeating the per-round perturbation that prevents counter-replay (Day-1 bug originally fixed at this site).
newly_uncovered_violations.fix: Replace with case `*N) ns=$(...)` cascade, OR call portable_fs `pfs_now_ns()` and slice last 9 chars.

newly_uncovered_violations.consequence_pre_fix: Same entropy-collapse on resonance seed perturbation.

axis_correlation_finding: detector-correctness and integration-justification are largely orthogonal. Heuristic tuning (guard token list, lookahead) had no bearing on tool placement; tool placement decision (standalone) was driven by existing lint surface specialization, not by the heuristic's nature.

evidence.F1: selftest unsafe synthetic: violations=3 (matches expected 3 unsafe forms).
evidence.F2: selftest safe-cascade form (python3 leg in window): not flagged.
evidence.F3: selftest safe-N-guard form (.contains('N')==false; case *N)): not flagged.
evidence.F4: selftest sentinel: __DATE_N_GUARD_LINT__ PASS violations=3 false_positives_in_selftest=0 files_scanned=2.
evidence.F5: regression on 21 audit-fixed files: __DATE_N_GUARD_LINT__ PASS violations=0 files_scanned=21.
evidence.F6: integration_decision section justifies standalone over lint_annot/py_ban_guard/atlas_*_lint extension.

carry_forward: Fix the 2 newly-uncovered run.hexa violations (lines 583, 604) — entropy-collapse on macOS for per-round/resonance salt. Apply same case *N) cascade or migrate to pfs_now_ns().
Wire `tool/date_n_guard_lint.hexa --staged` into .git/hooks/pre-commit alongside the existing py_ban_guard staged-file checks.

Optional: extend tool/hexa_only_lint.hexa (the canonical staged-files lint per repo policy, not yet present in tree at /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool — file did not exist) to AGGREGATE date_n_guard_lint sentinel into its own __HEXA_LINT__ output line. Defer until hexa_only_lint.hexa is created.

Audit raw .sh scripts (tool/falsifier_health.sh, tool/atlas_index.sh) flagged in parent cycle's carry-forward #1; they remain out-of-scope under raw#8 hexa-only edit policy and are not covered by this lint (which scans .hexa only).

files_touched.purpose: silence doc-prose at lines 577, 1014 (true-positive bugs at 583, 604 left flagged for carry-forward)
